explanation: The Sigma rule explicitly restricts detection to the process creation of the 'schtasks.exe' binary (Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'). By utilizing native PowerShell cmdlets like Unregister-ScheduledTask, the attacker interacts directly with the Task Scheduler API under the PowerShell process, completely evading this specific process creation rule.

output: copy C:\Windows\System32\schtasks.exe C:\Windows\Temp\task_updater.exe && C:\Windows\Temp\task_updater.exe /delete /tn "\Microsoft\Windows\BitLocker\BitLocker Encrypt All Drives" /f
explanation: The detection logic rigidly relies on the executable name ending with 'schtasks.exe'. Copying the legitimate schtasks.exe binary to an alternative directory with a different filename allows the execution of the exact same command-line arguments while bypassing the 'Image' selection criteria.
